In the poker scenario, the author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. To calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards, we use the complement rule. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from the deck is C(52, 5). The number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts is C(36, 5). Therefore, the probability of getting at least one heart is 1 - [C(36, 5) / C(52, 5)]. This simplifies to 1 - [1,021,642 / 2,598,960] ≈ 0.226.
